title: eriogonum shockleyi subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum shockleyi subsp. typicum, also known as Shockley's buckwheat, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum shockleyi subsp. typicum include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant, typically made from its leaves and stems, can be found in tea or tincture form, with some commercial products also available as capsules or topical creams.
